# Questions in Livestream 109

Live Stream #109: A Revised View of Pseudoscience

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=428" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:08</a> | Did Egyptians during the Late Period learn to speak Greek, and how did they communicate with foreigners about trade, armies, and intelligence? | He said Egyptian scribes learned diplomatic languages, including Greek in the Late Period, and served as interpreters for rulers, commanders, and diplomats. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=596" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:56</a> | Why did the Minoans import so much from ancient Egypt? | He said Egyptian goods and symbols were prestigious, useful, and culturally attractive, so Minoan borrowing was partly simple desirability. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=730" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:10</a> | Would a full manuscript of Manetho or Berossus significantly change our picture of Egypt or Mesopotamia? | He said it would clarify major historiographical questions, especially disputed passages, but not revolutionize everything by itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=918" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:18</a> | What are his thoughts on Catholicism? | He said Catholics are Christian brothers and sisters even where he disagrees with specific doctrines. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=1284" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:24</a> | Are there any early-date Exodus proponents he actually likes? | He named Randall Price as someone he likes personally and respects in some work, while still strongly disagreeing with Price's Exodus dating. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=1538" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">25:38</a> | Has he thought about interviewing other scholars? | He said he has considered it, but interviewing does not fit his strengths as well as teaching, answering questions, and making those formats engaging. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=1704" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:24</a> | Is there evidence that pharaohs Griffith treats as concurrent ruled the same city, and would that undermine the city-dynasty hypothesis? | He said yes; overlapping administrative centers, biographies, and synchronistic data make the city-dynasty hypothesis untenable. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=2019" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:39</a> | What are his thoughts on Christopher Rollston's interview with Sean McDowell? | He praised Rollston's evidential approach, agreed that the Mount Ebal amulet lacks visible writing, and criticized attempts to treat weak and strong views as equal. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=2797" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:37</a> | Are there any developments with the Amorite tablet from the Manasseh/Mana machine alert? | He said no new developments had appeared since the full publication, but he expected future work to test the grammar against other inscriptions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=2944" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:04</a> | Does the lack of Genesis 1-11 references in early prophetic texts imply those chapters are late? | He said no; absence of mention is not positive evidence for late composition. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=3235" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">53:55</a> | Does he know PhD reading lists for Egyptology or Old Testament self-study, and are online MAs a path to becoming a scholar? | He discouraged online MAs for scholarship because graduate study depends on relationships and recommendations; he said Old Testament reading lists are easier to find than Egyptology ones. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=3655" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:55</a> | What are his thoughts on historian David Miano and World of Antiquity? | He said he had not watched David Miano or World of Antiquity, then contrasted that with channels like Metatron that he had seen and liked. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=3906" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:06</a> | Did Hike badly misinterpret Tertullian's `Against Praxeas` by treating 1 Corinthians 15:28 as saying the Trinity will cease? | He said that would not surprise him, because Tertullian is often misread when readers lose track of whether Tertullian or an opponent is speaking. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=4511" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:11</a> | Is there any way to show the Bible hints that Adam's 930 years means the age of a clan rather than one individual? | He pointed to patriarchal ages, the Abraham/Terah problem, Exodus 6, and the Old Testament's focus on clans as evidence for a clan-identity reading. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=5378" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:38</a> | What does the `tribute of people` in Numbers 31:40 mean? | He read it in the context of war spoils and the Midianite campaign, not as proof of temple prostitution. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=5859" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:37:39</a> | Will the Mount Ebal tablet be the beginning of the end for ABR? | He said no; ABR's support base is church and donor networks, so academic embarrassment does not necessarily threaten the organization. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=6209" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:43:29</a> | What are good books for starting research on Egyptian chronology and Middle Eastern synchronisms? | He recommended Hornung and Warburton's Egyptian chronology work and emphasized primary sources such as Mesopotamian chronicles and Amarna letters. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=6460" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:47:40</a> | When should laymen trust the academic consensus, and when should they not? | He said consensus grounded in evidence deserves more trust, while consensus resting mainly on theory or interpretation should be weighed cautiously against the data. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=6639" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:39</a> | Would the documentary hypothesis fall into the category of interpretation rather than direct data? | He said yes; it is a theory built on interpretations, and he thinks some of its middle premises fail against the data. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=7136" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:58:56</a> | Is there proof that Shamash is Zedek, Kittu, or Osiris? | He said the question was unclear and could not be answered directly, while noting that local deities can arise independently or be folded into larger pantheons. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=7280" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:01:20</a> | Is Franck Goddio for real, or is he another Ron Wyatt? | He said he did not know enough about Goddio or Heracleion to judge, but underwater cities can be real when earthquake subsidence or similar processes lower land. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=7499" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:04:59</a> | What was the process of starting a new cult in Egypt, who was allowed to do it, and how was a new deity welcomed into the religious structure? | He said new cults usually developed from existing traditions, relationships, or divine attributes, and kings had the authority to install divine images in temples. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=7972" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:52</a> | Did monotheism, or something close to it, exist in cultures besides Israel? | He distinguished monolatry, henotheism, and monotheism, saying true monotheism first appears with Israel while other cultures often come closer to monolatry. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=8299" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:19</a> | What about native African religions and monotheism? | He said some may get close, but the examples he had encountered looked more monolatrous than clearly monotheistic, and he avoided a definitive claim outside his expertise. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=8526" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:22:06</a> | Why were pharaohs worried about peasant revolts caused by priests choking the food supply if peasants lacked military skill and technology? | He said priests could affect food supplies for both peasants and the military, and kings sometimes broke up priestly or regional land power to keep control centralized. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=8933" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:53</a> | On the clan-interpretation view, what does `begot` mean? | He said it means a line of descent, not necessarily an immediate father-son relationship, because Hebrew genealogies can skip generations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=9145" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:32:25</a> | How can local churches, especially more fundamentalist-leaning ones, become more interested in scholarly research and academics? | He recommended starting with poetry and symbolism to loosen a rigid literal-or-error mindset before moving into more contested scholarly questions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=9836" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:43:56</a> | Why would the king of Shinar go all the way to the Dead Sea for a raid if the trip cost exceeded the loot value? | He said Mesopotamian armies looted everything along the route, so the campaign was not only about the final target and its costs were offset by accumulated plunder. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=10123" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:48:43</a> | Would Egypt have been strong enough to fight the Mesopotamian looters if they headed to Egypt? | He said Egypt under the 13th Dynasty, especially under strong kings such as Neferhotep I and Sobekhotep IV, likely could have repelled them. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=10626" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:57:06</a> | Why are there no reported clashes between the Israelites and Egyptian mining parties in Sinai? | He said the Israelites likely took a different route while the mining operations were small, localized, and away from their main path. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=11047" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:04:07</a> | Were decorative and lewd graffiti like those at Hatshepsut's temple common in ancient Egypt, and what did common people think of her? | He said graffiti was widespread after temples were abandoned; common opinion of Hatshepsut is mostly unknown, while Thutmose III's monument damage was state-sponsored and personal. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=13034" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:37:14</a> | How long before the Rollston interview is taken down like Falk's debate was? | He thought it probably would not be taken down, because doing so after criticism of the Mount Ebal amulet would look like a cover-up. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=13318" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:41:58</a> | What is his opinion of `Crystal Blade`? | He had not read the novel, though he noted that the cover used his artwork and that fans of the book like it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=13898" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:51:38</a> | Have more conservative-leaning scholars written responses to the Levite hypothesis? | He said most responses he had seen were in the `Five Views` book or in book reviews, because conservative scholars are less likely to engage that hypothesis directly in journals. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/rson-uLBhPk?t=14144" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:55:44</a> | If the Hyksos numbered around 500,000 and Egypt had about 2.1 million people around 1600 BCE, why did that influx not collapse Egypt's food supply? | He said the population number is contested, the Delta and Faiyum were highly productive, and the Hyksos presence grew over centuries rather than arriving all at once. |
